content.problemStatement: You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Find the maximum value of the expression ∣Ax−Ay∣+∣Ay−Az∣+∣Az−Ax∣|A_x-A_y| + |A_y-A_z| + |A_z-A_x|∣Ax​−Ay​∣+∣Ay​−Az​∣+∣Az​−Ax​∣ over all triples of pairwise distinct valid indices (x,y,z)(x, y, z)(x,y,z).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N (the length of the array), the second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible value of |Ax‑Ay| + |Ay‑Az| + |Az‑Ax| over all triples of distinct indices.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case the answer is 2*(max-min). Test1: max=7, min=2 → 2*5=10. Test2: all equal → 0. Test3: max=5, min=2 → 2*3=6.

solution.approach: For any three numbers a, b, c let m = min(a,b,c) and M = max(a,b,c). The expression |a-b|+|b-c|+|c-a| simplifies to 2*(M-m). Therefore the maximum over all triples is achieved by using the global minimum and global maximum of the whole array, and any third distinct index. Hence answer = 2 * (global_max - global_min). The algorithm scans the array once to find min and max, then computes the formula.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, as we only perform a single linear scan.
